title: We are mistakenly seeing repos as registries.
body:
Currently `podman pull rhel7/rhel-tools` is failing because it
sees rhel7 as a registry.  This change will verify that the returned
registry from the parser is actually a registry and not a repo,
if a repo it will return the correct content, and we will pull the image.

Fixes https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1378

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@mtrmac is not going to like this, but I think we need to get this in for todays build.

@vrothberg @baude @mheon PTAL

> @mtrmac is not going to like this

Actually fixing this has been on my refactor to-do list.  If you have enough confidence that this is not going to break anything, go for it.

I’ve started working on <s>this</s> **EDIT the API necessary to do this in c/image** in https://github.com/mtrmac/image/tree/reference-additions , but APIs in the `c/image/docker/reference` package really need to never change semantics, and I am not confident enough about the design.

So, until the `reference-additions` branch is finished, this PR is perfectly fine with me.

Well, except that it breaks tests — but that’s fine, I have added these tests to document existing behavior and to prevent accidental changes, not to claim that the behavior is correct. AFAICS the failing tests should just be adjusted with the current values, but that will be easier to see with an updated PR.

> , but I think we need to get this in for todays build.

(_This approach_ I don’t like. If you are serious about weekly releases and discover a problem added in the last week, don’t rush in unfinished fixes, and last-minute stress, just because it is Friday; either revert the offending commit if it is new, or just release with the same bug as last week and take the necessary time to fix the bug properly.)
@mtrmac the reason I am rushing the fix in, is for the forthcoming RHEL beta release, not just to get it fixed for the weekly release.

> the reason I am rushing the fix in, …

Ah, I’m sorry, I didn’t realize the timing.
At least some of the test failures seem real - `docker:///ns/busybox:latest` doesn't seem like a correct path, I think?

> At least some of the test failures seem real - `docker:///ns/busybox:latest` doesn't seem like a correct path, I think?

Right, that’s invalid, but it’s not new — https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/4961001b7c94298199a049e59a8aaf3f1c74a696/libpod/image/parts_test.go#L28-L32 shows the same behavior.

It turns out that `.assemble` and `.assembleWithTransport` are only called if `parts.registry != ""` (though not only when `!parts.hasRegistry`, strictly speaking, because the caller is editing the structure), so it does not really hurt right now.

Longer-term I’d like for `parts` to disappear and to use mostly `reference.Named` directly, but that again depends on having a stable API for the “does the name specify a registry” heuristic.

For now, maybe just mark the updated test with the same
```go
// FIXME? The [empty]/ns/busybox syntax is surprising.
```
comment.

:pushpin: Commit 78f421c has been approved by `mtrmac`
